Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp) To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiffs Angelo D Lima And Juliane Smith
Off Calendar Mar-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT VERTEX PROPERTY GROUP Notice Of Motion And Motion Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 (Anti-Slapp) To Strike The Complaint Of Plaintiffs Angelo D Lima And Juliane Smith is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. "At the first step, the moving defendant bears the burden of identifying all allegations of protected activity, and the claims for relief supported by them. If the court determines that relief is sought based on allegations arising from activity protected by the statute, the second step is reached." Baral v. Schnitt (2016) 1 Cal.5th 376. Defendant's Notice of Motion while claiming that the motion seeks to strike "specified claims" from the complaint, does not actually specify any claims (See Notice of Motion). The Points and Authorities appear to specify the claims as "breach of contract" and "nuisance" (Points and Authorities 8:10-11) and identify the allegations (Points and Authorities 2:14-4:1). Based on the language chosen to identify these allegations as "claims," the moving party appears to conflate the two.
Reading the Notice and Points and Authorities as a whole, Defendants have met their burden of identifying the allegations and the claims for relief arising therefrom only as to the claim for breach of contract which in its entirety arises out of protected activity. (Complaint, paragraph 51, "posting the 10-day notice on Plaintiff's door."). Plaintiffs failed to carry their burden to show of prevailing on the merits. Therefore, the claim for breach of contract arising out of the service of the Notice is stricken.
Defendants failed to meet their burden to identify the claims for nuisance arising from protected activity. ANTI-SLAPP is framed by the pleadings, claims of nuisance are not based on any protected activity identified by the Defendants. (Complaint, paragraph 82 and 88). It is Defendants burden under the first step of the ANTI-SLAPP to tie the allegations to the claims, for nuisance claims Defendants failed to do so.
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)
